Zonghan Li
PhD Student
University of Toronto

I am Zonghan Li, a PhD student and researcher at the Psychometrics and Responsible AI Lab, University of Toronto, supervised by Dr. Feng Ji.

I study how generative AI is changing the way we measure, intervene in, and understand human behavior. On one side, I design and test LLM-powered personalized interventions, exploring whether and how they can shift the choices people make in everyday life. On the other, I take a critical look at LLMs as research tools. When we use them to simulate participants, generate stimuli, or build scales, when can we trust them, and when do they quietly mislead us? My work cuts across field experiments, computational modeling, and methodology, and I hope it contributes to a more reliable and responsible set of practices for behavioral research.

I'm also drawn to behaviors where there's a weak link between attitude and action, e.g., pro-environmental and health behaviors. I trace how social events and public policies shape these behaviors, and how these individual-level choices, in turn, aggregate into system-level consequences.

I received my M.Sc. in Engineering from Tsinghua University and B.Econ. in Applied Economics from China University of Petroleum.

I'm always happy to chat with people working on LLM applications in behavioral simulation and intervention. Please feel free to reach out via email if you'd like to collaborate or just talk through ideas.

zonghan.li(at)outlook.com Google Scholar LinkedIn

News
2026
🎙️Two abstracts accepted to Canadian Psychological Association (CPA) 2026 Annual Convention at Montréal, June 4–6. Will present work on (1) leveraging GenAI for behavioral interventions through digital nudges, and (2) designing personalized behavioral interventions with large language models.
Feb 10
2025
🏆 Our paper published in Scientific Data has been awarded the Capital’s Frontier Academic Achievement Award by the Beijing Association for Science and Technology (BAST).
Nov 26
📑 Three papers published! (1) Environmental Impact Assessment Review—modeled COVID-19 protective behaviors across pandemic stages using survey data; (2) Resources, Environment and Sustainability—quantified long-term emissions pathways for urban water systems and evaluated mitigation scenarios; (3) Building and Environment—linked citywide noise-complaint patterns to neighborhood built-environment features.
Jun 24
🎓 I graduated from Tsinghua University! Thrilled to be an Outstanding Graduate of Beijing and excited to join University of Toronto as a doctoral student!
Jun 23
2024
🎤 Presented my poster on Mining the potential of large languange models on nudging daily energy and water conservation at AGU24 in Washington D.C.
Dec 02
🏆 Awarded National Scholarship for two consecutive years (the only master’s student among the 2021–2024 cohorts at School of Environment).
Oct 02
🎤 Attended the International Conference on Applied Energy in Niigata, Japan and presented my work orally.
Sep 06
2023
🏆 Awarded "Best Young Scientist Oral Presentation (first class)" at the International Conference on Resource Sustainability!
Aug 06
📄 Our dataset on household energy efficiency of mainland China is published at Scientific Data!
Jul 02
